title: cordyline terminalis var. sepiaria
intro: Cordyline terminalis var. sepiaria, also known as Red Dracaena or Cabbage Palm, is a popular ornamental plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. Cordyline terminalis var. sepiaria can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
